entry.title: Re: Periode verschieben für Buchungskreis in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear @Benedikt_Pantoja,

Yes for Public Cloud the correct way to shift the period is open the next period / close the old one is:

FI (G/L, AP, AR) posting periods:

Use Manage Posting Periods (App ID F2293) to open/close FI posting periods. SAP explicitly states posting period status is maintained with this app

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/cdccca8e03d74101a0135863bc522b49/3a06c5536a51204be10000000a174cb4.html

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2293')/S35

If you can’t open/close periods, it is often role/authorization related. SAP provides a dedicated SAP Note for Manage Posting Periods authorization

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003308006

Important Public Cloud nuance:

  • In Public Cloud, FI period control is driven by the Posting Period Variant (PPV) assigned to the company code
  • In the standard delivery SAP generally has one PPV per company code, so changing the PPV effectively changes that company code
  • Since CE2502, SAP also supports assigning multiple company codes to one PPV so  t’s meaning your change affects all company codes sharing that PPV

SAP Notes below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3598811

If you can’t open/close periods, it’s usually authorization/role related there is a dedicated SAP note about F2293 authorizations

SAP Notes below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0002598676

CO (Controlling) posting periods:

For CO period locks (by business transaction types, etc.) the Public Cloud standard is Manage Posting Periods Cost Accounting (App ID F4684), not F2293

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/044eb1694b564390a6479c62497b6bc1.html

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F4684')/S35

MM / Inventory (goods movements) posting periods:

Use Close Periods (MM period) SAP explains this app controls the open MM posting period and typically keeps current/previous open

More details below:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/how-works-the-close-periods-app-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud/ba-p/13549042

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-sourcing-and-procurement/opening-new-posting-period_e58522bf-3ba2-4b88-a896-efbe59973965

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: MRP Planned Orders Dates & Detailed Material coverage
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Could you please advise where we can configure the system so that, when executing MRP, planned orders can be created with past requirement dates or adjusted opening dates?

Currently, when planned orders are generated with the current date, customers find it difficult to prioritize which planned order should be processed first. If the system could propose dates in the past (in line with requirement dates), it would help clarify priorities.

Additionally, we would like to better understand how the opening date is determined — particularly the time interval between converting a planned order and the actual production start date.

Furthermore, could you recommend a report or CDS view or app similar to MD04 that provides detailed time-phased information for all products, including:

  • Total requirements
  • Available stock
  • Purchase/production orders
  • Quantity shortages by period

The Monitor Material Coverage app only displays the first projected shortage and does not provide a complete time-phased overview and you should go product by product to see the details.

Thank you in advance for your guidance.

Best regards,

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Supply Chain 

 

@Lakshmipathi  @Markus_Oertelt @Caetano

entry.title: SAP Variant Configuration & Pricing Integration with SAP Cloud Application Lifecycle Management-CALM
entry.content:
Why Integrating SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing with SAP Cloud ALM Matters More Than Ever

SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing integrates with SAP Cloud Application Lifecycle Management (SAP Cloud ALM) and enables using the following features:

  • Integration Monitoring
    You can view messages from the following components centrally:
    • Variant Configuration service
    • Pricing service
    • Data replication
  • Real User Monitoring
    You can see the performance of API calls to the following services:
    • Variant Configuration service
    • Pricing service

Select Scope and Select Service Type - SAP Variant Configuration & Pricing

1. Achieve Centralized Monitoring Across Your Landscape

SAP Cloud ALM provides a central cockpit where teams can monitor communications across VCP components. According to current standard process, the following are fully monitored in one place:

  • Variant Configuration service
  • Pricing service
  • Data replication 
Having this single point of truth eliminates the need to shuffle between logs, systems, or custom monitoring tools. It improves operational efficiency and drastically reduces troubleshooting time.

2. Detect Integration Issues Faster with Integration & Exception Monitoring

Integration processes involving VCP often pass through multiple cloud services, external systems, and business applications. Any delay or failure in message flow can disrupt order processing, pricing calculations, and product configuration consistency.

With Cloud ALM:

  • Messages to and from VCP are captured and classified.
  • Integration exceptions can be filtered by status, configuration IDs, document IDs, and additional parameters.
  • IT teams/VCP Experts in your team can rapidly isolate root causes.

SAP’s VCP monitoring confirms that Integration & Exception Monitoring includes category‑specific insights for VCP, helping identify message failures or anomalies early. 

4. Enable Proactive Operations Through SAP‑Managed Connectivity

Moving data into Cloud ALM doesn’t require custom middleware or manual telemetry setup. VCP uses SAP‑Managed Connectivity—SAP’s built‑in OpenTelemetry‑based infrastructure.

This means:

  • Setup is streamlined; only monitoring activation is required.
  • Data flow to Cloud ALM is secure and compliant.
  • Continuous telemetry is provided with minimal administrative overhead.

SAP Help documentation provides you steps that activating monitoring in Cloud ALM apps is enough to begin data collection. 

5. Strengthen Your Digital Transformation with Lifecycle Transparency

Integration with Cloud ALM is not just about monitoring—it is about operational excellence.

By linking VCP and Cloud ALM, organizations gain:

  • Full visibility into operational health
  • Insights across the entire configuration and pricing chain
  • Support for agile, cloud‑first lifecycle management

As SAP Cloud ALM continues to evolve as the central operations and lifecycle tool for SAP cloud solutions, integrating VCP ensures your configuration and pricing processes are aligned with SAP’s roadmap. The integration setup documentation explicitly highlights these monitoring scenarios.

6. A Must‑Have for Production Security Teams: Proactive Detection of Errors, Threats, and API Anomalies

In a production environment, security and reliability become non‑negotiable. Integrating SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing (VCP) with SAP Cloud ALM empowers security and operations teams to continuously monitor for:

  • Failed or suspicious API calls
  • Warnings or error messages spikes
  • Unexpected behavior in configuration or pricing service interactions
  • Patterns that may indicate threats or misuse

SAP Cloud ALM’s Integration Monitoring allows central visibility into messages exchanged across the Variant Configuration service, Pricing service, and data replication components. This helps teams quickly identify anomalies, failed message exchanges, or abnormal traffic patterns. 

Additionally, Real User Monitoring surfaces performance characteristics of API calls to both services, including latency issues or unusual load, which can serve as early warning signs of security or stability problems. 

Together, these capabilities give your security team the observability needed to:

  • Detect potential threats originating from abnormal API traffic
  • React quickly to production errors before they escalate
  • Maintain compliance and audit readiness
  • Strengthen the system’s overall security posture

In highly dynamic configuration and pricing scenarios—where APIs are called hundreds or thousands of times per minute—this level of monitoring is not just beneficial; it is essential for protecting your production environment.

Conclusion: A Small Integration with a Big Impact

Connecting SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing with SAP Cloud ALM is more than a technical convenience—it is a strategic enabler. Organizations benefit from:

  • Faster issue detection and resolution
  • Better performance insights
  • Enhanced transparency
  • Simplified lifecycle management
  • Improved customer experience

Whether you're managing complex configurable products or ensuring accurate pricing across global channels, this integration provides the operational control needed to keep everything running smoothly.

 

Watch out for the next blog for SAP Variant Configuration & Pricing integrated with SAP Cloud ALM benefits.

entry.title: Exploring and Configuring Joule Agents in SAP CX: A Three‑Part Video Series
entry.content:
The rapid evolution of AI‑driven automation is redefining how organizations deliver customer experiences. Within SAP Customer Experience, Joule agents represent one of the most significant shifts—bringing intelligence, autonomy, and business context into everyday workflows across Sales and Service Cloud.
To help teams understand what agents can truly achieve in practice, I created a three‑part video series that walks through both the functional possibilities and the technical foundation behind configuring and extending agents in SAP CX. This series offers a clear and practical view into the end‑to‑end lifecycle of agent usage, from real business scenarios to hands‑on customization.
You can access the full playlist here:
https://learning.sap.com/video-playlists/exploring-and-configuring-joule-agents-in-sap-cx

Why Agents Matter Right Now

Organizations are under increasing pressure to respond to customers faster, personalize interactions, and remove friction from sales and service processes. Yet, many teams still struggle with manual tasks, disconnected systems, and inconsistent execution.
Joule agents address these challenges by operating as intelligent digital teammates. They can understand context, execute tasks, orchestrate data across systems, and guide users through complex steps. The goal is not to replace human expertise but to elevate it—enabling more time for meaningful customer engagement.
This video series was designed to make this vision concrete. Instead of broad theory, the sessions focus on scenarios that matter to frontline teams and show exactly how agents can be shaped to fit unique business needs.

What the Three‑Part Series Covers

1. Real Business Use Cases for Agents in Sales and Service Cloud

The first video explores where agents provide the highest impact across customer‑facing roles. Through practical examples, it demonstrates how agents can support tasks such as lead qualification, opportunity preparation, service case triage, knowledge retrieval, and guided troubleshooting.
This session sets the stage by showing why these capabilities matter and how they align with the daily challenges of sales and service organizations.

2. Customizing a Standard, Pre‑Delivered Agent

The second video takes a step deeper by breaking down the components of a pre‑delivered agent. It shows how to adapt and configure standard behavior to match organizational processes, without the need for heavy development.
From modifying prompts and adjusting workflows to tuning business logic, this video highlights how quickly teams can tailor agents to be more relevant and more effective.

3. Building a Brand‑New Custom Agent

The final video in the series focuses on creating a custom agent from the ground up. This includes defining the agent’s purpose, shaping its actions, setting up integrations, and ensuring it responds the right way in the right contexts.
It provides a complete view of the technical architecture behind agent customization, giving both business and technical audiences a clear blueprint for building their own intelligent automation assets.

Who This Series Is For

This content is designed to support solution consultants, implementation teams, architects, and business leaders who want to move beyond conceptual discussions and truly understand what agents can deliver in real processes.
If your organization is preparing for an adoption journey, a customer demo, or a deeper technical exploration, these videos provide a foundation you can build on right away.

Final Thoughts

AI‑driven agents are rapidly becoming core to SAP’s customer experience strategy. As their capabilities expand, so will the expectations from customers and internal teams alike. Understanding how to use, adapt, and build agents now will help organizations stay ahead of that curve.
I hope this series serves as a practical guide and inspires new possibilities for automation in your sales and service workflows.
Here is the playlist again for easy access:
https://learning.sap.com/video-playlists/exploring-and-configuring-joule-agents-in-sap-cx

entry.title: Re: Physical Stock Negative
entry.content:
First off :

  • Did the system generate a ShortDump, an ErrorMessage or anything as a starting point for your issue?
    If so, please provide the info ... (But assuming it did not ...)
  • Was a basic check via Prgr. "/SCWM/ERP_STOCKCHECK" performed?
    What was the outcome?

Next :
Have you checked for SAP-KBA's on your issue?
Please consider the following suggestions :
3079956 - CHM: Physical stock inconsistencies with negative quants - SAP for Me
3362742 - /scwm/r_lime_consistency_check - report to scan and correct LIME inconsistencies - SAP for Me
3629852 - Superfluous /SCWM/QUAN entry created in storage type that allows negative stock - SAP for Me
1915719 - Correction report for physical stocks - SAP for Me
3204028 - LIME consistency check report and must to have notes - SAP for Me
3588458 - There are inconsistencies in stock between IM and EWM - SAP for Me

From the above resources, you can derive a number of (analysis / correction) reports and suggestions. Check if Prgrs. such as "/SCWM/CORRECT_QUANTS" and "/SCWM/R_LIME_CONSISTENCY_CHECK" can be useful for ANALYSIS / DETECTION of the inconsistency. Please evaluate what these Reports do before executing them (you need to be sure the report only DISPLAYS data or also CORRECTS / UPDATES a table)!

If the above approaches did not provide any solution, please do as determined in SAP-KBA "3588458" :
"If the issue still cannot be resolved using the Compare Stock with Inventory Management app (= Prgr. "/SCWM/ERP_STOCKCHECK"), raise a ticket to SAP under the component SCM-EWM-IF-2CL."

Hope this helps

Nic T.

entry.title: How to Clean Up Media Table and Blob Files on SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
Problem Statement

Creating an excessive number of entries in the Media table can introduce several critical issues across the SAP Commerce Cloud platform. The most common impacts include:

Database over consumption

  • A high volume of Media records reduces the Flexible Search cache hit ratio, especially for queries frequently executed against the Media table.

  • As a result, the database must process significantly more media-related queries, increasing overall database consumption and slowing down the environment.

Slower PDP Rendering

  • Product detail pages (PDPs) rely on components like ProductGaleriesImagePopulator and ProductPrimaryImagePopulator, which fetch product images from the Media table.

  • When the table contains millions of media items, these populators take longer to resolve images, directly impacting PDP load time and customer experience.

CronJob and Deployment Slowdowns

  • A large number of CronJob LogFile media leads to:

    • Slow CronJob startup

    • Slow deployments

    • System Update delays

  • In several cases, deployments became stuck because the system needed to evaluate thousands of tiny CronJobs. The bottleneck wasn’t the CronJob execution itself, but the repeated checks on whether each CronJob could run—each check triggering a query on LogFile media.

Data Backups taking hours to take and restore

  • Millions of Media entries imply millions of small files on Azure Blob Storage.

  • Snapshot or backup operations must process all these files, often resulting in hours or even days of backup time, slowing maintenance windows and increasing operational risk.

Over-usage of Cloud Storage

The entitlement of storage account is limited to 5 TB. Reducing the number of medias will help to reduce the number of blob files associated to these media.

Common Root Causes of Excessive Media

The following patterns typically lead to runaway media growth:

  • CronJob Logging - Overly verbose CronJob logging, especially SystemUpdate or Solr indexing, can account for up to 99% of all LogFile media in some systems.
  • ImpEx Import / Export - Each ImpEx import or export can generate temporary media items unless properly cleaned up.
  • Invoices - Invoices stored in SAP Commerce Cloud and not archived.
  • Emails - Email Messages not removed after sending and never cleaned up.
  • Inbound / Outbound Integrations - Integrations that log every inbound or outbound request into Media generate a large number of temporary files.
  • Product Media Explosion - Large catalogs, multiple image formats, and multiple catalog versions together multiply the number of product images stored in the system.

Overview with Medias Statistics Analyzer Script

In this article, we provide a script that generates comprehensive metrics about media table use. The output is a CSV report with statistics on media count, size, and age by folder/type.

As the queries execution may be slow, running with Cronjob rather than directly in HAC avoid any timeout. 

Cronjob ImpEx  

Import the ImpEx script below with HAC.

INSERT_UPDATE Script; code[unique = true]              ; content                                   ; scriptType(code); active[default = true, unique = true];
                    ; media-statistics-analyzer-script ; "
                    import java.sql.DriverManager
import java.sql.ResultSetMetaData
import org.slf4j.Logger
import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
import java.text.SimpleDateFormat
import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets

def LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(""MediaStatisticsAnalyzer"")

def conf = configurationService.getConfiguration()

def dbUrl      = conf.getString(""db.url"")
def dbUser     = conf.getString(""db.username"")
def dbPassword = conf.getString(""db.password"")
def dbDriver   = conf.getString(""db.driver"")

def SLEEP_BETWEEN_QUERIES_MS = 5_000   // 5 seconds

def queries = [
       [
                title: "Medias with data stored count",
                sql: '''
                SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p_datapk) AS unique_count FROM medias
        '''
        ],
		[
                title: "Medias Deletion count",
                sql: '''
                SELECT COUNT(*) FROM mediadeletions
        '''
        ],
		[
                title: "Media count by folder",
                sql: '''
            SELECT
                mf.p_qualifier AS qualifier,
                COUNT(m.pk)    AS count,
                ROUND(SUM(m.p_size) / 1024.0 / 1024 / 1024, 0) AS Size_GiB
            FROM medias m
            JOIN mediafolders mf ON mf.pk = m.p_folder
            GROUP BY mf.p_qualifier
            ORDER BY count DESC
        '''
        ],
		[
                title: 'Media count by folder (modified in last 24 hours)',
                sql: '''
            SELECT
                mf.p_qualifier AS qualifier,
                COUNT(m.pk)    AS count,
                ROUND(SUM(m.p_size) / 1024.0 / 1024 / 1024, 0) AS Size_GiB
            FROM medias m
            JOIN mediafolders mf ON mf.pk = m.p_folder
            WHERE m.modifiedts > DATEADD(HOUR, -24, GETUTCDATE())
            GROUP BY mf.p_qualifier
            ORDER BY count DESC
        '''
        ],

        [
                title: 'Media count by folder (older than 6 months)',
                sql: '''
            SELECT
                mf.p_qualifier AS qualifier,
                COUNT(m.pk)    AS count,
                ROUND(SUM(m.p_size) / 1024.0 / 1024 / 1024, 0) AS Size_GiB
            FROM medias m
            JOIN mediafolders mf ON mf.pk = m.p_folder
            WHERE m.modifiedts < DATEADD(MONTH, -6, GETUTCDATE())
            GROUP BY mf.p_qualifier
            ORDER BY count DESC
        '''
        ],

        [
                title: 'Media count by type',
                sql: '''
            SELECT
                ct.internalcode AS code,
                COUNT(*)        AS count,
                ROUND(SUM(m.p_size) / 1024.0 / 1024 / 1024, 0) AS Size_GiB
            FROM medias m
            JOIN composedtypes ct ON ct.pk = m.typepkstring
            GROUP BY ct.internalcode
            ORDER BY count DESC
        '''
        ],

        [
                title: 'Media count by type (last 24 hours)',
                sql: '''
            SELECT
                ct.internalcode AS code,
                COUNT(*)        AS count,
                ROUND(SUM(m.p_size) / 1024.0 / 1024 / 1024, 0) AS Size_GiB
            FROM medias m
            JOIN composedtypes ct ON ct.pk = m.typepkstring
            WHERE m.modifiedts > DATEADD(HOUR, -24, GETUTCDATE())
            GROUP BY ct.internalcode
            ORDER BY count DESC
        '''
        ],
		[
                title: 'Media count by type (last 6 months)',
                sql: '''
            SELECT
                ct.internalcode AS code,
                COUNT(*)        AS count,
                ROUND(SUM(m.p_size) / 1024.0 / 1024 / 1024, 0) AS Size_GiB
            FROM medias m
            JOIN composedtypes ct ON ct.pk = m.typepkstring
            WHERE m.modifiedts > DATEADD(MONTH, -6, GETUTCDATE())
            GROUP BY ct.internalcode
            ORDER BY count DESC
        '''
        ],

        [
                title: 'Images per format',
                sql: '''
            SELECT
                mf.p_qualifier                     AS folder,
                COALESCE(fmt.p_qualifier, 'NO_FORMAT') AS format,
                COUNT(*)                           AS count
            FROM medias m
            JOIN mediafolders mf ON mf.pk = m.p_folder
            LEFT JOIN mediaformat fmt ON fmt.pk = m.p_mediaformat
            WHERE mf.p_qualifier = 'images'
            GROUP BY mf.p_qualifier, COALESCE(fmt.p_qualifier, 'NO_FORMAT')
            ORDER BY count DESC
        '''
        ]
]

def closeQuietly = { obj -> try { obj?.close() } catch (ignored) {} }

def connection
def statement
def resultSet

def now = new Date()
def timestampStr = new SimpleDateFormat(""yyyyMMdd_HHmmss"").format(now)
def mediaCode = ""media-statistics-report-${timestampStr}""
def realFileName = ""media-statistics-report.csv""

def baos = new ByteArrayOutputStream()
def writer = new OutputStreamWriter(baos, StandardCharsets.UTF_8)

LOG.info(""Starting CSV generation for media statistics report"")

try {
    DriverManager.registerDriver(Class.forName(dbDriver).newInstance())
    connection = DriverManager.getConnection(dbUrl, dbUser, dbPassword)
    statement  = connection.createStatement()

    queries.eachWithIndex { q, idx ->
        LOG.info(""Starting query {}/{} : {}"", idx + 1, queries.size(), q.title)
        long start = System.currentTimeMillis()
        resultSet = statement.executeQuery(q.sql)
        ResultSetMetaData meta = resultSet.getMetaData()
        int columnCount = meta.getColumnCount()

        // Write query title and timestamp
        writer.write(""Query: ${q.title}\n"")
        writer.write(""Timestamp: ${new SimpleDateFormat(""yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"").format(new Date())}\n"")

        // Write headers
        def headers = (1..columnCount).collect { meta.getColumnLabel(it) }
        writer.write(headers.join("","") + ""\n"")

        // Write rows
        int rowCount = 0
        while (resultSet.next()) {
            def row = (1..columnCount).collect { col ->
                resultSet.getObject(col)
            }
            writer.write(row.join("","") + ""\n"")
            rowCount++
        }
        long duration = System.currentTimeMillis() - start
        LOG.info(""Query {}: {} rows written | Execution time: {} ms"", idx + 1, rowCount, duration)

        // Empty line to separate queries
        writer.write(""\n"")

        closeQuietly(resultSet)

        if (idx < queries.size() - 1) {
            LOG.info(""Sleeping {} ms before next query"", SLEEP_BETWEEN_QUERIES_MS)
            Thread.sleep(SLEEP_BETWEEN_QUERIES_MS)
        }
    }

    writer.flush()
    LOG.info(""CSV generation completed, size: {} bytes"", baos.size())

    // Save as Media
    def mediaService = spring.getBean(""mediaService"")
    def modelService = spring.getBean(""modelService"")
    def media = modelService.create(""Media"")
    media.setCode(mediaCode)
    media.setRealFileName(realFileName)
    media.setMime(""text/csv"")
    media.setFolder()
    modelService.save(media)
    mediaService.setStreamForMedia(media, new ByteArrayInputStream(baos.toByteArray()))
    modelService.save(media)
    LOG.info(""Media saved: PK={}, code={},  Download link= {} "", media.pk, media.code, media.getDownloadURL())
    println ""Media saved: PK= ${media.code} : code= ${media.pk} : Download link= ${media.getDownloadURL()}""

} catch (Exception e) {
    LOG.error(""Error executing SQL queries or generating CSV"", e)
} finally {
    closeQuietly(resultSet)
    closeQuietly(statement)
    closeQuietly(connection)
    closeQuietly(writer)
    closeQuietly(baos)
}

LOG.info(""All queries executed and CSV media generated successfully"")
println ""All queries executed and CSV media generated successfully""

                                        " ; GROOVY          ; true


INSERT_UPDATE ScriptingJob; code[unique = true]           ; scriptURI(code)                          ; useReadOnlyDatasource
                          ; media-statistics-analyzer-job ; model://media-statistics-analyzer-script ; true


INSERT_UPDATE CronJob; code[unique = true]                ; job(code)                     ; sessionLanguage(isoCode)[default=en]; active
                     ; media-statistics-analyzer-cron-job ; media-statistics-analyzer-job ;                              ; true

Execute the Cronjob

  • Search for media-statistics-analyzer-cron-job in backoffice
  • The Cronjob is created but not scheduled
  • Set Language for CronJob, check the box to execute on the replica DB and run it preferably during low traffic

How to monitor 

Filter on the logger name MediaStatisticsAnalyzer in CLS to monitor the job progress:

  • Filter in Backoffice log
  • Search for "MediaStatisticsAnalyzer" 

When the job is completed, copy the report location.

Download the output

Result is saved into a media with the name media-statistics-report-<timestamp>

Here is a sample of CSV File generated:

✅ Tip ✅ Identify the top media folders and media types and conduct the deep-dive analysis focus on these ones that you should clean-up in priority.

Deep-dive analysis

Deep-dive Image analysis

Total number of images per catalog version

SELECT 
    COALESCE({c:name}, COALESCE({c:id}, 'NO_CATALOG'))        AS catalog,
    COALESCE({cv:version}, 'NO_VERSION')    AS catalogVersion,
    COUNT(*)                                AS count
FROM 
    { Media AS m
      JOIN MediaFolder AS f       ON {m:folder}         = {f:pk}
      LEFT JOIN CatalogVersion AS cv ON {m:catalogVersion} = {cv:pk}
      LEFT JOIN Catalog        AS c  ON {cv:catalog}       = {c:pk} }
WHERE 
    {f:qualifier} = 'images'
GROUP BY 
    COALESCE({c:name}, COALESCE({c:id}, 'NO_CATALOG')),
    COALESCE({cv:version}, 'NO_VERSION')
ORDER BY 
    count DESC

✅ Tip ✅ Moving images to Catalog unaware can drastically help to reduce the number of medias: https://community.sap.com/t5/crm-and-cx-blogs-by-sap/enhancing-scalability-of-your-product-catalog-with-catalog-unaware-media/ba-p/13762371

Images per format

SELECT 
    mf1.p_qualifier AS Qualifier,
    COALESCE(mf2.p_qualifier, 'NO_FORMAT') AS Format,
    COUNT(*) AS Count
FROM medias m
JOIN mediafolders mf1 
    ON mf1.PK = m.p_folder
LEFT JOIN mediaformat mf2     -- LEFT JOIN to include medias without format
    ON mf2.PK = m.p_mediaformat
WHERE mf1.p_qualifier = 'images'
GROUP BY 
    mf1.p_qualifier,
    COALESCE(mf2.p_qualifier, 'NO_FORMAT')
ORDER BY 
    Count DESC

✅ Tip ✅ Validate that all formats are required and used on the frontend. It often happens that some formats are imported by not used. 

Product count per catalog version

SELECT 
    COALESCE({c:name},  COALESCE({c:id}, 'NO_CATALOG') )          AS catalog,
    COALESCE({cv:version}, 'NO_CATALOGVER')   AS catalogVersion,
    COUNT(*)                                   AS count
FROM 
    { Product AS p
      LEFT JOIN CatalogVersion AS cv ON {p:catalogVersion} = {cv:pk}
      LEFT JOIN Catalog        AS c  ON {cv:catalog}       = {c:pk} }
GROUP BY 
     COALESCE({c:name},  COALESCE({c:id}, 'NO_CATALOG') ) ,
    COALESCE({cv:version}, 'NO_CATALOGVER')
ORDER BY 
    count DESC

 ✅ Tip ✅ The objective here is to estimate the number of images per product to understand if there is anything wrong.

Product count by approval status

SELECT {ArticleApprovalStatus:code} AS Status, COUNT(*) AS Count
FROM {product
          JOIN ArticleApprovalStatus ON {product:approvalstatus} = {ArticleApprovalStatus:pk}}
GROUP BY {ArticleApprovalStatus:code}

✅ Tip ✅ By reducing the number of products, we look for the opportunity to reduce the number of images. 

Deep-dive cronjob logs analysis

Here is the query to identify cron jobs that are generating too much logs and executing more than once.

SELECT
  COALESCE({cj:code}, '<null>') AS "Cron Job",
  COUNT({l:pk}) AS "Total",
  MIN({l:modifiedtime}) AS "Oldest",
  MAX({l:modifiedtime}) AS "Newest" 
FROM
  {LogFile AS l 
  LEFT JOIN
    CronJob AS cj 
    ON {l:owner} = {cj:pk} } 
GROUP BY
  {cj:code} 
HAVING COUNT({l:pk}) > 1
ORDER BY
  "Total" DESC

The platform does not clean up old log files out-of-the-box. If you have cronjobs that run very frequently (for example, every few minutes), you should schedule the log file cleanup even more frequently. Running the cleanup more frequently avoids building up too many log files that need to be deleted.

✅ Tip ✅ Disable logging for Cronjob where this is not necessary in particular for the jobs that are generating too much logs.

✅ Tip ✅ Delete old Cronjob logs and set up a retention job using cleanUpLogsJobPerformable using the sample ImpEx script shared in Data Maintenance And Cleanup blog post > Cronjob logs.

Deep-dive catalog sync logs analysis

To be completed

✅ Tip ✅ Create a retention job for old catalogsync logs medias. See sanecleanup extension for cleaning up Commerce section.

Deep-dive ImpEx Medias analysis

Here is the query to identify ImpEx Media (generated).

SELECT
  COUNT(*) 
FROM
  {ImpexMedia AS i} 
WHERE
  (
    {i:code} LIKE '0_______' 
    OR {i:code} LIKE 
      'generated impex media - %' 
  )

Every ImpEx import or export generates at least one ImpexMedia. These media stay in the system, the platform does not delete them when it deletes the ImpEx jobs they belonged to (ImpEx media can potentially be re-used for other ImpEx jobs, but that's rarely ever the case). 

✅ Tip ✅ Create a retention job for Media using Data Retention Framework and the sample ImpEx script shared in Data Maintenance And Cleanup blog post > ImpexMedia.

Deep-dive Inbound request analysis

SELECT
    {InboundRequest.type} AS type,
    COUNT(*) AS count
FROM
    {InboundRequest}
WHERE 
    {Payload} IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY
    {InboundRequest.type}
ORDER BY
    count DESC
 
select {starttime}, {active} from {Cronjob} where {code}='inboundRequestCleanupCronJob'

 

✅ Tip ✅ Check ifinboundRequestCleanupCronJob is enabled and run recently.

Deep-dive Email Body and Email Attachment analysis

EmailMessage represents a single email that can be sent through the platform.

EmailBody extending Media is a persistent type that stores the actual email content, usually when the body is generated dynamically or is too large to store directly on EmailMessage (attribute called bodyMedia).

EmailAttachment extending Media represents a single attachment associated with anEmailMessage (attribute called attachments).

Once an email is sent in SAP Commerce Cloud, the EmailMessage, EmailBody and EmailAttachment items are not discarded or consumed. They remain persisted in the database as a historical record and only used for:

  • Audit and traceability

  • Debugging and support

  • Reviewing what was actually sent

  • Retry if the email failed

✅ Tip ✅ EmailMessage, EmailBody and EmailAttachment can be safely cleaned up. If you need to keep for audit and traceability, you can export and archive into an external system.

Email by business process

This query will help you to identify the top business processes creating EmailMessage.

SELECT {bp.processDefinitionName} AS 'Business Process', count(*) AS 'Count'
FROM {EmailMessage AS em JOIN BusinessProcess AS bp ON {em.process} = {bp.pk}}
GROUP BY {bp.processDefinitionName}
ORDER BY count(*) DESC

✅ Tip ✅ If the emails are sent with OOTB sendEmailAction step in a BusinessProcess, then ensure you call OOTB removeSentEmail step to clean up sent emails and their bodyMedia and attachment automatically.

<action id="sendEmail" bean="sendEmail">
	<transition name="OK" to="removeSentEmail"/>
	<transition name="NOK" to="failed"/>
</action>

<action id="removeSentEmail" bean="removeSentEmail">
	<transition name="OK" to="success"/>
	<transition name="NOK" to="error"/>
</action>

Emails By EmailType

This query will help you to identify the top types of EmailMessage.

SELECT {et.code} as 'Type', count(*) as 'Count' 
from {emailmessage as em join emailtype as et on {em.emailtype} = {et.pk}}
group by {et.code}
order by count(*) desc

High Level Media deletion analysis

In the media deletion table, we will understand how many media files have been soft deleted since the last execution of purgeDeletedFilesCronJob.

It helps to understand if the purgeDeletedFilesCronJob is enabled and what are the files folders that have been recently deleted (The default threshold for purging media deletions is managed by the property PurgeDeletedFilesJob.minAgeInHours which is set to 48 hours)

SELECT
    p_folderqualifier AS Folder,
    COUNT(*) AS Count
FROM mediadeletions
GROUP BY
    p_folderqualifier
ORDER BY
    Count DESC

✅ Tip ✅ If there is a large amount of medias to cleanup, the OOTB purgeDeletedFilesCronJob may be very slow (sometimes weeks) to clean-up the files because it runs with only one single thread. Here is a blog article that provides true multithreading solution to delete large number of media blobs: https://community.sap.com/t5/crm-and-cx-blog-posts-by-sap/efficiently-purging-large-number-of-deleted-media-blobs/ba-p/14239077.

entry.title: Re: Close button greyed out in SAP for me
entry.content:
To close a support case, it is necessary for an S-user to have the Close Cases authorization.

This authorization can be assigned by any administrator who holds the Close Cases authorization. To request this authorization, you may reach out to any of the administrators listed at https://me.sap.com/userscontacts/impcont. Alternatively, you can follow the self-service process outlined in KBA 2891528. 

For more detailed information. please refer to 2889234 - Unable to close a support case - SAP for Me

If you have any questions or need further assistance, please feel free to contact us. 

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Group Reporting - Change of Local currency from Functional Currency to Company code Currency
entry.content:
Hi SAP Expert Team,

Present SAP setup in group reporting at version  level Local currency source is blank and is used across all consolidation units in the SAP system. That means at consolidation unit level local currency is assigned with Functional currency by default.

As per the change in reporting requirements in S4HANA, we would like to change Functional currency to a different currency for some of the entities. We do not want to carry this change to Group Reporting and would like to switch to company code currency in Group Reporting at version level across all consolidation units so that the local currency at consolidation unit will continue to be the same as earlier but source will shift from FC to CC.

 

Can we freely do this change in GR version in any period since we are not changing the currency but changing the source.

We are preparing to change the historical data in S4HANA for the units where FC is changed, which may not have impact in GR if we shift to FC to Company code currency

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting - Change of Local currency from Funct...
entry.content:
Dear 

The issue regarding your request to change the Local Currency (LC) for the consolidation version . To address your inquiry:  

1. It is possible to change the LC for an existing version.  
2. However, based on the guidance provided in the Local Currency Change documentation Local Currency Change  , it is recommended to perform this action during the first period of the fiscal year.  

I understand that your consolidation version includes multiple units with FC=CC. For safety and accuracy, it is advisable to make this change in the first period of the year and subsequently run the BCF process to ensure consistency across all units.  

 

Best Regards,

Jerry

entry.content:
I would like to configure monitoring for the "HANA Memory Size" metric to monitor the Cloud ALM HANA memory (24GB) limit.

When I set the "Threshold Setting" Type to "Numeric" for the Cloud ALM metric "HANA Memory Size,"
it seems to be configured as an application-level alert setting, such as "Health Monitoring" or "Integration Monitoring."

I would like to monitor the entire HANA memory in Cloud ALM, rather than monitoring by application.




------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Cloud ALM HANA Memory Monitoring
entry.content:
Hi Customer，

Greetings from SAP CALM team.

As per outlined in KBA ##3432729 - How to Monitor SAP Cloud ALM Memory Usage, please be aware of the following contents:

  • Note: the thresholds maintained for the Hana Memory Size metric apply per use case. This means that the thresholds are considered on a per use case basis, not based on total memory consumption.

At present, it is not possible to set a threshold for the HANA Memory Size metric for the entire HANA memory in Cloud ALM.

Sincerely,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Deleted goals still appear on the home page
entry.content:
Hi Farook,

To remove the corrupted goal cards, currently it's necessary to delete all the goal cards. But you may regenerate the correct ones afterwards.

The steps to resolve the issue are as follows:
1. Disable goal card (takes around 1hr)
2. Delete all goal card following the steps in Managing the Home Page - Disabling or Enabling Home Page Cards
3. Enable Goal Card (takes around 1hr)
4. Go to Manage Templates and open the goal plans that are generating cards on users' homepage
5. Check the General Settings page of the goal plan and make sure that the due date is not passed. Because when you click the Refresh button (which is beside "save as") to regenerate goal card, it only regenerate goals for goal plans that are not passed their due date.
For example,
The goal plan due date is March 1 2026. If you click the refresh button after March 1 2026, no card will be regenerate.
If you click the refresh  button before March 1 2026, correct cards will be regenerated.

Best regards,
Yilin
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Mass scraping in sap after approval
entry.content:
Why Quality Notifications alone won’t be enough

QM Notifications are designed for defect recording, corrective/preventive actions, and quality issues. They can capture approvals and workflow steps, but they don’t natively integrate with mass stock scrapping across plants and material types. You’d end up with fragmented records if you try to use QM notifications as the single source of truth for inventory disposal.

Recommended Harmonized Process

1) Stock Identification & Grouping
Use ADGI (Adjust Stock in General Inventory) or physical inventory / stock type reports to identify leftover stock. Group by plant + material type using custom reports or CDS views.

2) Approval Workflow
Trigger a workflow (via Flexible Workflow or BPM) when grouped stock is flagged for disposal. Approval routing can be based on value thresholds (price) and frequency (monthly cycle). This workflow record becomes the single source of approval evidence.

3) Execution of Scrap
Once approved, execute mass scrapping using standard transactions (e.g., MB1A with movement type 551 for scrap, or EWM scrapping process). In decentralized EWM, scrapping is triggered from ERP and replicated to EWM, ensuring warehouse consistency.

4) Audit Trail
Link the workflow approval record to the material document created during scrapping. This way, you have one harmonized chain: stock identified → approval → scrapping posting.

Alternatives / Enhancements

Custom Fiori App: Build a lightweight app that consolidates leftover stock, triggers workflow, and posts scrap once approved.

Use of ADGI with Workflow: ADGI can handle adjustments, but you’d need workflow integration to capture approvals.

Central Record: Instead of QM notifications, use Workflow logs + Material Documents as the single source of truth. This is cleaner and aligns with compliance needs.


Best Practice

  • Keep scrapping execution in ERP/EWM standard processes.
  • Use workflow for approvals rather than QM notifications.
  • Ensure reporting layer (CDS view or custom report) consolidates approvals and postings for audit.

entry.title: Where can I obtain the updated S/4HANA Public Cloud SSCUI document?

entry.title: Re: Unassigned Cost Center despite assignment in 1~ Stand...
entry.content:
Good day @rcirujano_us 

Thank you for your question.

In Public Cloud, you can only activate Standard hierarchy 1 when the Unassigned Master Data list is empty. So, if any cost centers are unassigned, you will not be able to activate it.

If it still shows as unassigned even though you assigned it already, then the cause could be:

  • The cost center and the hierarchy node must overlap by validity dates. If the node is valid from a later date, the cost center is still considered unassigned for the activation date. You can solve this by:
    • Opening the node and checking its valid from-valid to timeframe in the Manage Global Hierarchies app.
    • Ensuring the node validity covers the cost center validity.
  • You assigned it in the cost center master, but the hierarchy still has a draft. If hierarchy 1 is being edited, the system can still show items as unassigned. To solve this:
    • Ensure you save the hierarchy first, thereafter, activate it in the Manage Global Hierarchies.
  • Alternatively, there is a multiple hierarchy concepts that are mixed up. The reason is, in some landscapes there is confusion between cost center groups and global hierarchies. Note that the standard hierarchy is handled as a global hierarchy for new customers and is maintained via Manage Global Hierarchies.

The following references provide more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f0d042517a434458b171ff5867b18445/8e040a45e4db4e6abd426bee0a86dc8a.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c56f622a2edf491b9f1b596b55587009/e98a67e25fd246408db8c39d8d0b6175.html?locale=en-US

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3594285

 

Here is a quick guide in your system:

In Manage Global Hierarchies,

open Cost Center Standard Hierarchy 1, go to the Unassigned Master Data, confirm CA10100000 is available.

Still in Manage Global Hierarchies, select the cost center and assign it, ensure you choose the target node under hierarchy 1, save and activate it.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Blank page on MPH when external candidates are moved...
entry.content:
Hi @Riife ,

I understand that you are using the RCM–EC integration while bypassing Onboarding. One possible reason for this issue is that the candidate may have already been hired and was subsequently moved back manually to the Ready to Hire status. Please note that it is not possible to initiate the hiring process again for the same Application ID.

To proceed, you may either create a new job requisition to rehire the candidate or create a new candidate profile and have them apply to the same requisition. This will generate a new Application ID, allowing the hiring process to move forward without blockage.
Regards,

Akarsh

entry.title: Re: MRP Planned Orders Dates & Detailed Material coverage
entry.content:
Here’s how this works in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition:

1. Planned Orders with Past Requirement Dates

Standard MRP behavior:
Planned orders are created with the current date if the requirement date lies in the past. This is by design — MRP does not propose “late” dates, it assumes you should act immediately.

Configuration options:

You cannot configure MRP to generate planned orders with past requirement dates. Instead, you can influence the opening date and start date via in-house production time, planned delivery time, and scheduling parameters in the material master. These settings determine how far in advance MRP opens the planned order relative to the requirement date.


2. Opening Date Determination

The opening date is calculated as:

Opening Date = Requirement Date -In-house Production Time

  • If you convert a planned order to a production order, the system schedules backward from the requirement date using lead times (setup, processing, teardown).
  • The interval between conversion and production start depends on:
    • Scheduling parameters (forward/backward scheduling).
    • Lead times maintained in the routing/master recipe.
    • MRP control parameters in the material master.

3. Time-Phased Overview (MD04 Equivalent)

In Public Cloud, MD04 (Stock/Requirements List) is not available. Instead, you can use:

  • Monitor Material Coverage app: Shows shortages but only the first projected shortage.
  • Manage Material Coverage app: Provides more detail but still not fully time-phased.
  • CDS Views:
    • Stock/Requirements List (I_MaterialStockRequirement)
    • Material Coverage (I_MaterialCoverage)
    • These can be consumed in custom analytical apps or reports to replicate MD04-like functionality.

entry.title: Re: Fields Changed in RDP Templates
entry.content:
Basic Fraction Functions were introduced in June 2025.
They were added to the data model, as certain reports, like Danish EPR, required information about barriers, coatings and additives.

This information is even more granular than the basic fractions, which was the highest level of granularity, supported by RDP before the change.

When changes are made to the data model, the Excel templates are updated.

You can always take the latest templates from the Import Data app.

Regarding the other observation - about fields becoming mandatory for Spain EPR...

This must have been updated when Spain EPR Ecoembes was released in the end of 2024.

Service packaging and compostable packaging is in scope of Ecoembes report, that's why these attributes are mandatory.

More details about the rules and the usage of each attribute is available in the report documentation, which can be accessed from the RDP launchpad

entry.title: Re: Compliance form did not trigger for external candidat...
entry.content:
Hello @Riife ,

The compliance forms in onboarding is triggered with the help of FieldMetaOverrideConfiguration within Manage Data. If the workstate and workcountry within FieldMetaOverrideConfiguration is configured via Job Information > Location > Address, you need to ensure that the location value is passed from RCM at the time of initiation, or the location is maintained during New Hire Data Review step. If it is configured in via any other path, please ensure that the field value configured within this path has a valid value. 

Regards,

Akarsh

entry.title: Good to know: "HASH Partitioning" vs "RANGE Partitioning" in context of SAP HANA database
entry.content:
• Why was this blog post created?
  • What are the differences?
  • Where can I find the most important information about the topic of SAP HANA partitioning?
  • Complexity
  • What is the conclusion?
  • Other articles
  • Do you have further questions?
  • Contribution

 

last updated: 2026-03-04

Why was this blog post created?

The area of partitioning is a very complex topic when it comes to the SAP HANA database (in case of all kind of database). The blog post aim is to summarize the facts and explain the different types on high level (pro, cons). Deeper understanding is available in the referenced documents. A high-level understanding is a critical step in order to choose the correct and most effective partitioning in your actual need/situation.

What are the differences?

The following tables contains the differences.

 HASHRANGE
Amount of dataLess data.Huge amount of data.
When to use?Solves only the 2 billion limit.Next to partitioning (2 billion limit) there are further requirements (e.g. performance needs).
FocusFocus is only the partitioning itself.Focus is not only the partitioning.
ImplementationEasy setup.Preparation and planning necessary.
Implementation TypePure technical task.Planning & Technical tasks.
InvolvementNo need other participants (only DBA).Need further teams (e.g. application knowledge).

 

Where can I find the most important information about the topic of SAP HANA partitioning?

SAP Knowledge Base Article(s):

  • 2044468 - FAQ: SAP HANA Partitioning
  • 3146645 - What is the best approach in partitioning tables on SAP HANA?
  • 3307500 - How to decide which partitioning type and column(s) should been used to partition a table in SAP HANA?

SAP Community Article:

  • Collected information regarding partitioning in SAP HANA (with examples)

Complexity

The complexity is also indicated by the fact that all relevant information can be found in the central documentation (2044468), however, based on experience, further explanations are needed for the interpretation and use of this documentation, which is why additional supporting documents were created (e.g. 3307500). Furthermore, the Blog listed above was created to present specific examples and to compile the supporting documents.

What is the conclusion?

Understanding is the key element to do the proper partitioning of the table based on different requirements. This document explains a top down approach and gives an overview about the topic and the necessary documentations.

Other articles

✍️ Where can I find knowledge and information belongs to SAP HANA?
✍️ Where can I find information about the available tools for SAP HANA (all types of use)?

Do you have further questions?

Please do not hesitate to contact me if you have question or observation regarding the article.
Q&A link for SAP HANA: https://answers.sap.com/tags/73554900100700000996 

Contribution

If you find any missing information belongs to the topic, please let me know. I am happy to add the new content. My intention is to maintain the content continuously to keep the info up-to-date.

Release Information

Release DateDescription
2026.03.04First/initial Release of the SAP Blog Post documentation (Technical Article).

entry.title: Partial stock transfer of QI stock
entry.content:
Hi Guys,

following situation:

I post the PGR of 10 hobbocks in our external warehouse(A) which runs into QI-stock.

For a quality inspection i want to move only 1 hobbock with a stock transfer to my warehouse(B) as best all quantities remains the whole time in QI. An UD is obligated at the end.

Its all within the same plant, only 2 different stor.locs.

At the moment i have two options: 

1. A complete pre-release of the inspection LOT to unrestricted and moving (MIGO) 1 hobbock from "A" to "B", but then the whole quantity is in unrestricted although its not physically QI-approved and could be used by mistake. This is the current procedure.

2. Use the QAC2 to move the complete quantity based on the LOT while its in QI, but thats makes no sense for my issue, because i need just 1 hobbock in "B".

Maybe s.o. have a smart and easy solution to handle this.

Thanks in advance and br,

Simon

entry.title: Re: Elimination of SAP PI PO systems
entry.content:
Hi,

Recently. I think the need to migrate to SAP  integration suite has been mentioned the last 5 years. 

I do think SAP PI/PO does make a lot of integration easier, and you don't really have the same ease of use in cloud integration for reliable messaging. 

And it would be nice if you did not need to migrate from either PI or from ECC, and just keep running the integration forever like Business Connector. 

But there are also scenarios that are overly complicated to make in SAP PI, and that does not really make it easy to monitor. 

And it does not really fall into a modern architecture, which makes it easy to add and support new use cases. 

I do think there are good option and patterns that makes it easier to perform reliable messaging like the pipeline flows. 

And you can probably also find your own patterns to support the way you think integration works. 

 

Daniel Graversen /Figaf

entry.title: Re: Inbound France eInvoice via DRC/PEPPOL not arriving i...
entry.content:
France-Specific Considerations

Chorus Pro vs. PEPPOL:
France mandates Chorus Pro for B2G invoices, and for B2B the rollout is tied to the new reform (2026–2027). Even though PEPPOL is supported, inbound invoices must comply with French-specific schemas and identifiers. If the inbound invoice doesn’t carry the correct SIREN/SIRET or Chorus Pro-compatible identifiers, the ERP may reject it silently.

Participant Identification:
In test setups, if sender and receiver share the same identifiers, the inbound routing may fail. France requires strict validation of the buyer’s identification numbers (SIREN/SIRET, VAT ID). Using identical IDs for sender and receiver can block inbound posting.

Inbound Mapping:
SAP DRC requires inbound mapping to be configured for France-specific formats. If the inbound XML is pushed but not mapped to an ERP document type, it will show as “Document pushed” in PEPPOL Exchange but never appear in eDocument Cockpit.

What to Check

  • Identifiers: Ensure the customer/vendor master data has correct French identifiers (SIREN/SIRET, VAT ID). Avoid using the same IDs for sender and receiver in test.
  • Inbound Mapping: Verify that inbound mapping for France is active in DRC customizing. Check if the inbound XML is being rejected due to missing fields.
  • DRC Logs: In SAP BTP PEPPOL Exchange, “Document pushed” means the document reached the tenant. Check the DRC inbound logs to see if it was rejected during mapping.
  • SAP Notes: Review SAP Notes under FI-LOC-FR / LO-DRC-FR for inbound invoice handling. SAP has published notes clarifying inbound requirements for France.

entry.title: Re: Material Quality Rejected should show as Rejected in...
entry.content:
For information, below are the Standard Stock types in SAP

  • Unrestricted stock → available for consumption/production.
  • Quality inspection stock → held until a usage decision is made.
  • Blocked stock → not usable, typically for rejected materials.
  • Restricted‑use stock (in some industries) → usable but only under certain conditions.


How to differentiate your two cases

Case 1: Rejected during QC, to be returned
Keep using blocked stock. This aligns with SAP’s standard definition: unusable, pending return or disposal.

Case 2: Usable but not for all situations

  • Options:
    • Quality inspection stock: If the material is awaiting a decision, you can hold it here until released.
    • Custom stock type via special stock indicator: Many companies create a custom stock type (e.g., “restricted use”) by enhancing movement types and reporting.
    • Storage location segregation: Place these materials in a dedicated storage location flagged as “restricted.” This way, they show separately in MMBE/MB52 and can be excluded from consumption by customizing availability check rules.
    • Batch status management: If you use batch management, you can mark batches as “restricted” vs. “unrestricted.” Restricted batches cannot be consumed until status is changed.

Preventing Consumption

  • Availability check customizing: You can configure ATP so that restricted/blocked stock is not considered in MRP or consumption.
  • Movement type control: Allow postings only from restricted stock to unrestricted stock before consumption.
  • Process orders: Ensure BOM explosion and goods issue only pull from unrestricted stock.


Reporting

MMBE / MB52: With storage location or batch status segregation, you’ll see clear differentiation.

Custom CDS view / report: If you introduce a custom stock type, you can build a report to show “Rejected vs. Restricted” separately.
